1. List common characteristics of your best clients (hobbies, age, vacation preferences).  
2. Observe clients—don’t outsource the important stuff (how they make decisions, describe you, concerns about financial decisions, why they think you’re their advisor).  
3. Build a buyer persona: define an ideal client with objectives, quotes, personality, life story, and basic demographics.
List common characteristics of your best clients; observe clients; build a buyer persona.

The author gives three tips: 1) Line them up — list common characteristics of your best clients (hobbies, age, vacation preferences). 2) Observe your clients — don't outsource the important stuff (how they make decisions, how they describe you, what concerns them financially, why they think you're their advisor). 3) Build a buyer persona — define an ideal client (objectives, quotes, personality, life story, basic demographics).

Line them up: list common characteristics of your best clients (hobbies, age, vacation preferences); Observe your clients: understand how they make decisions, how they describe you, their financial concerns, and why they choose you; Build a buyer persona: define an ideal client by objectives, quotes, life story, personality, and basic demographics.
